statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a GIT branch name. The branch name should consist of alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores. It should not start or end with a hyphen or underscore. Write a function that takes a string as input and returns `true` if the string is a valid GIT branch name, and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the branch name
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the branch name is valid
statement.notes: The function should be case-sensitive and should not trim the input string.

constraints.description: The length of the branch name

examples.explanation: The branch name consists of alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores, and does not start or end with a hyphen or underscore.
The branch name starts with a hyphen, which is not allowed.
The branch name starts with an underscore, which is not allowed.

hints.text: Start by checking if the branch name starts or ends with a hyphen or underscore.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing GIT Branch Name Validation
editorial.intuition: The function should check if the branch name contains only allowed characters and does not start or end with a hyphen or underscore.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a loop to iterate over the characters in the branch name and check each character individually.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a regular expression to match the branch name pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match the branch name pattern.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression pattern `^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*[a-zA-Z0-9]$` matches any string that starts with a letter or digit and contains only letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores.
editorial.dryRun: You can use the dryRun option to test the function with different branch names.
commonMistakes: Not checking if the branch name starts or ends with a hyphen or underscore
Using a too permissive regular expression pattern
